title: Desktop Point of Sale (POS) Application
subtitle: An offline-first desktop cashier application built with Flutter for local retail businesses.
overview: Designed and built a desktop cashier (Point of Sale) application from scratch using Flutter, designed specifically as an offline-first operational solution for retail stores and local businesses.
responsibilities: Designed and built the desktop cashier (Point of Sale) application from scratch using Flutter, specifically customized as an offline-first operational solution for retail stores and local businesses.

Implemented a self-contained local monolith architecture, ensuring the application runs 100% without internet connection dependencies (standalone application) to guarantee cashier transaction stability.
Developed key features independently, including a responsive cashier system, real-time inventory stock management, automatic profit-loss report calculation, and thermal printer receipt integration.
Managed efficient local data storage using SQLite to ensure instant transaction data reading and writing processes with optimal memory consumption.
features: Responsive cashier system custom-tailored for retail desktop layouts.
Real-time inventory and stock tracking management.
Automatic profit-loss calculation for instant financial statements.

architecture.description: The system utilizes a local monolith architecture powered by SQLite embedded database. All transaction writes, reads, and reports aggregations are executed completely offline on the local device, guaranteeing instant operational speed and privacy.

challenges: Managing real-time inventory synchronization and profit-loss calculations on local SQLite storage without blocking the UI thread or consuming excessive memory during high-frequency checkout events.
solutions: Optimized SQL queries using proper database indexing and utilized Dart background threads (Isolates) to perform heavy financial report calculations asynchronously.
lessonsLearned: Learned the critical value of database optimization for offline-first local checkout transactions and memory management in Flutter-based desktop software.

title: Anime Watch Tracker System
subtitle: A cloud-synced multi-platform anime watching history tracker with Telegram bot integration.
overview: Designed and implemented an integrated monitoring platform composed of a Google Chrome Extension (watch-tracker) to capture real-time watching events on Netflix, YouTube, and custom streaming domains, a backend API service (awt-api) hosted on Vercel, and a Supabase database with automated cloud cron notifications.
responsibilities: Developed the Google Chrome Extension (watch-tracker) using TypeScript to inject event handlers into Netflix, YouTube, and generic HTML5 video players.
Built the serverless REST API (awt-api) using TypeScript and Node.js, deployed on Vercel, to receive telemetry reports periodically.
Integrated Supabase (PostgreSQL) database for local-first synchronization using a Universal Sync ID without requiring user accounts.
Created a Telegram Bot integration allowing users to link devices, view watching lists, and query upcoming episode schedules from AniList.
Configured Vercel Cron Jobs to check AniList schedules daily at 12:00 PM WIB and send notifications automatically to Telegram.
features: Automated video player event listener (Netflix, YouTube, custom streaming domains).
Universal Sync ID for passwordless, secure multi-device synchronization.
Telegram Bot integration for watching lists (/list), episode updates (/new), and AniList schedules (/schedule).
Automated daily digest check and notifications via Vercel Cron Jobs running 24/7.

architecture.description: The system employs a local-first architecture where the Chrome Extension acts as the primary offline cache. When enabled, it performs bi-directional synchronization with Supabase via a Vercel serverless API (awt-api). A Vercel Cron Job runs daily to handle background schedule checking and alerts via Telegram bot webhook integrations.

challenges: Creating a secure, multi-device synchronization protocol without requiring user registration while avoiding double notifications between browser extensions and serverless cron jobs.
solutions: Designed a Universal Sync ID generation system that serves as a unique PostgreSQL key. Built an activation flag to toggle off local browser background alarms when Cloud Cron is enabled on the server.
lessonsLearned: Mastered multi-platform browser DOM scripting, building secure serverless endpoints with PostgreSQL/Supabase, and scheduling automated tasks with Vercel Cron.
